---
title: "ACore"
description: |
  A Python package with statistical functions to analyse multimodal molecular data.
categories: 
  - 🛠️Software development
  - 🧫Omics
  - 🧬Bioinformatics
date: "2025-10-10"
draft: false
image: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Multiomics-Analytics-Group/acore/HEAD/docs/images/logo/acore_logo.svg
---

[{{< fa brands github >}} Source code][source-code]{.btn target=_blank}
[{{< iconify bi book >}} Documentation][docs-link]{.btn target=_blank}
[{{< iconify ion logo-python>}} Python package][python-package]{.btn target=_blank}

I contributed to this project as part of my work as research assistant at the [Multiomics Network Analytics Group (MoNA)][Mona] - [Novo Nordisk Foundation Center for Biosustainability (DTU Biosustain)][Biosustain].

## Summary

<p align="center">
  <img src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Multiomics-Analytics-Group/acore/HEAD/docs/images/logo/acore_logo.svg" width="350" title="acore_logo">
</p>

**ACore** is a Python package with statistical functions to **analyse multimodal molecular data**. It is designed to be flexible and extensible, allowing users to perform a wide range of analyses on **multi-omics datasets**. It is part of a **broader ecosystem of tools** for multi-omics analysis, working in conjunction with [VueCore][vuecore] and [VueGen][vuegen] to enable end-to-end data processing, visualization, and reporting.

This package is still in development, but it already includes functions for **data preprocessing, normalization, batch correction, enrichment analysis, and differential analysis** of multi-omics data.

## Additional information

The **ACore documentation** is available at [analytics-core.readthedocs.io][docs-link], where you can find detailed information of the package’s classes and functions, installation and execution instructions, and API usage notebooks for all analyses. The source code is openly available on [GitHub][source-code], where you can also find the latest updates and contribute to the project.

[source-code]: https://github.com/Multiomics-Analytics-Group/acore
[docs-link]: https://analytics-core.readthedocs.io/latest/
[python-package]: https://pypi.org/project/acore/
[Mona]: https://multiomics-analytics-group.github.io/
[Biosustain]: https://www.biosustain.dtu.dk/
[vuecore]: https://github.com/Multiomics-Analytics-Group/vuecore
[vuegen]: https://github.com/Multiomics-Analytics-Group/vuegen
